hero.label: BK-AERO-LAB / 2024
hero.tagline: Vietnam's exclusive TT & Triathlon performance lab. Not mass-market — precision-engineered for your exact geometry. Every millimeter. Every watt.

manifesto.label: OUR POSITION
manifesto.body: Mass-market brands build for the average rider. BIGKID builds for you. Your torso length, your flexibility, your race goals — every component we recommend is scored against your exact geometry before we ever suggest it.

precision.title: PRECISION
precision.body: Positional tolerance under 1mm. We measure reach, stack, and drop against your body — not a size chart.

performance.title: PERFORMANCE
performance.body: UCI-legal. Aero-verified. Every component in our lab is selected for real-world speed gains, not shelf appeal.

yours.title: YOURS ALONE
yours.body: Small batch. Handbuilt. No two setups are identical because no two riders are. This isn't off-the-shelf.

tolerance.label: Positional Tolerance
tolerance.sub: The difference between a perfect aero position and a wasted one.

watts.label: Potential Aero Gain
watts.sub: At 40km/h. The gap between your current setup and an optimised one.

steps.label: From Consult to Speed
steps.sub: Geometry. Match. Ride. The BIGKID process takes days, not months.

process.label: HOW IT WORKS
process.sub: No guesswork. No trial and error. The BIGKID process maps your exact geometry to the right product before anything ships.

geometry.title: Geometry Profile
geometry.body: Submit your torso, arm, inseam measurements and flexibility rating. Takes 3 minutes.

match.title: Lab Match
match.body: We score every product in our lab against your profile. Receive a ranked shortlist with real fit data.

consult.title: Direct Consultation
consult.body: A specialist reviews your profile and follows up. No bots. No forms. Real conversation.

finalCta.label: START HERE
finalCta.body: It starts with your geometry. Run the free fit tool or book a direct session with a BIGKID specialist — no commitment, no sales pressure.

fitting.title: Fit Tool
fitting.description: Enter your body geometry and select a product. The engine scores compatibility and surfaces actionable adjustments — no guesswork.

flexibility.1: 1 — Rigid, very limited range of motion

tooltips.torso: Measured nape-of-neck to top of pelvis (sitting upright). Determines ideal stack height — the vertical distance from BB to your hands.
tooltips.arm: Full arm length shoulder to wrist (arm straight). Drives reach calculation — the horizontal distance from BB to your hands. A 10mm error here shifts ideal reach by 9mm.
tooltips.inseam: Inner leg length floor to crotch. Sets saddle height and influences pad drop — the height differential between saddle and pads.
tooltips.flexibility: How freely you can rotate at the hip and hold an aero position. Restricted riders (1–2) receive a safety offset — ideal reach is reduced to prevent injury.
tooltips.aggression: Your target position intensity. A score of 5 targets maximum UCI-legal aero — lower scores dial in more comfort at the cost of some drag reduction.
tooltips.fitScore: A weighted score across Reach, Stack, and Drop compatibility. 8+ = race-ready. Below 6 = significant adjustments needed before ordering.

consult.title: Request a Consultation
consult.description: We don't sell off the shelf. Tell us what you're working towards — a specialist will reach out via Telegram or email.

fields.messagePlaceholder: Your race goals, current setup, pain points...

consult.successBody: We'll be in touch. Expect a response within 24 hours via the contact you provided.

aero.description: At 40km/h, aerodynamic drag accounts for over 80% of your power output. Select your current position and your target — see the real watt and time cost.
aero.disclaimer: Tropical mode uses ρ = 1.18 kg/m³ (warm, humid, sea level). Standard uses ISA 1.225 kg/m³. Yaw penalty adds +5% for real-world crosswind. Actual gains depend on rider position, equipment, and course.

tooltips.cda: CdA = Drag Coefficient × Frontal Area. Reducing CdA by 0.01 m² saves roughly 5W at 40 km/h — the margin between podium finishes.
tooltips.density: Air density drops in heat and humidity — tropical air is ~4% less dense than ISA standard. Less dense air means less drag at identical speed.
tooltips.yaw: At race pace, crosswinds create a 5–15° yaw angle that increases effective drag. Enabling this adds a realistic +5% penalty — making your savings estimate conservative.
tooltips.wattsSaved: Watts freed from aerodynamic drag at your selected speed. At identical power output, this translates directly to higher speed.
tooltips.timeSaved: Time advantage over the selected distance assuming identical power output. Every second counts on the clock.
